text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of your code without changing its outside behavior. Imagine you have a messy room. Sorting and organizing it doesn't change what's in the room, but it makes it easier to find things. Refactoring works the same way: it makes your code cleaner and more readable. This helps you and other developers understand the code faster. Over time, messy code creates "technical debt," which is like a loan you have to repay with extra effort later. Regular refactoring helps you pay off this debt, making future changes easier and reducing the risk of bugs.
